posts.title: Welcome to Seattle Emergency Hubs
posts.author: Seattle Emergency Hubs Team
posts.excerpt: We're excited to launch our new website! Learn about our mission to build neighborhood resilience through community hubs and mutual aid networks.

posts.title: Build Your 72-Hour Go Bag
posts.author: Preparedness Committee
posts.excerpt: A step-by-step guide to assembling a 72-hour emergency kit for yourself and your household. Being ready starts at home.

posts.title: What Is an Emergency Hub?
posts.author: Outreach Team
posts.excerpt: Emergency hubs are neighborhood gathering points staffed by trained volunteers. Find out how they work and how your neighborhood can start one.

posts.title: Spring Training Day Recap
posts.author: Training Team
posts.excerpt: Over 60 volunteers attended our spring training event at Rainier Community Center. Read highlights and how to join the next session.

posts.title: Earthquake Preparedness Tips for Seattle Residents
posts.author: Preparedness Committee
posts.excerpt: Seattle sits near several active fault lines. Here are the essential steps every household should take to prepare for a major earthquake.

posts.title: How Mutual Aid Works After a Disaster
posts.author: Outreach Team
posts.excerpt: Mutual aid is neighbors helping neighbors. We explore real-world examples and how Seattle Emergency Hubs facilitates this network of care.
